heading: Conservation loan and loan guarantee program
label: 1924 Conservation loan and loan guarantee program

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary may make or guarantee qualified conservation loans to eligible borrowers under this section.

children.heading: Qualified conservation loan
children.label: 1 Qualified conservation loan
children.text: The term “qualified conservation loan” means a loan, the proceeds of which are used to cover the costs to the borrower of carrying out a qualified conservation project.

children.heading: Qualified conservation project
children.label: 2 Qualified conservation project
children.text: The term “qualified conservation project” means conservation measures that address provisions of a conservation plan of the eligible borrower.

children.label: A
children.text: the installation of conservation structures to address soil, water, and related resources;

children.label: B
children.text: the establishment of forest cover for sustained yield timber management, erosion control, or shelter belt purposes;

children.label: C
children.text: the installation of water conservation measures;

children.label: D
children.text: the installation of waste management systems;

children.label: E
children.text: the establishment or improvement of permanent pasture;

children.label: F
children.text: compliance with ; andsection 3812 of title 16

children.label: G
children.text: other purposes consistent with the plan, including the adoption of any other emerging or existing conservation practices, techniques, or technologies approved by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may make or guarantee loans to farmers or ranchers in the United States, farm cooperatives, private domestic corporations, partnerships, joint operations, trusts, limited liability companies, or such other legal entities as the Secretary considers appropriate that are controlled by farmers or ranchers and engaged primarily and directly in agricultural production in the United States.

children.heading: Requirements
children.label: 2 Requirements
children.text: To be eligible for a loan under this section, applicants shall meet the requirements in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of .section 1922(a)(1) of this title
qualified beginning farmers or ranchers and socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers;
owners or tenants who use the loans to convert to sustainable or organic agricultural production systems; and
producers who use the loans to build conservation structures or establish conservation practices to comply with .section 3812 of title 16

children.heading: Limitations applicable to loan guarantees
children.label: e Limitations applicable to loan guarantees

children.text: 80 percent of the principal amount of the loan; or
in the case of a producer that is a qualified socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher or a beginning farmer or rancher, 90 percent of the principal amount of the loan.

children.heading: Administrative provisions
children.label: f Administrative provisions
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure, to the maximum extent practicable, that loans made or guaranteed under this section are distributed across diverse geographic regions.

children.heading: Credit eligibility
children.label: g Credit eligibility
children.text: The provisions of paragraphs (1) and (3) of shall not apply to loans made or guaranteed under this section.section 1983 of this title

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: h Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out this section $150,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2014 through 2023.
